author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary

patch.value: Adopt ItemMetadata SDK reader after pm-cli terminology migration

Replace pm-changelog imports and documentation of the removed listAllFrontMatter SDK export with listAllItemMetadata so the published extension activates against pm-cli's canonical TOON metadata API and pm-cli release gates remain ecosystem-safe.

message: Created after duplicate-safe all-status search. Existing SDK integration items are closed historical work or track unrelated missing-dependency behavior; this issue is the new pm-cli API rename compatibility fix discovered by pm-cli PR #531 Gates (static).

author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary
message: Started compatibility fix from pm-cli PR #531 static-gate evidence.

author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary

value.note: Adopt canonical pm SDK metadata reader

message: Linked source and generated package artifacts for the compatibility fix.

author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary

value.note: Package build and 108-test suite against local pm-cli PR SDK

message: Linked package verification and release readiness gates.

value.author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary
value.text: Evidence: pm-cli PR #531 Gates (static) failed because published pm-changelog imported removed listAllFrontMatter. Updated source and built extension artifacts to listAllItemMetadata and item metadata wording; installed the local pm-cli PR package without saving dependency changes; npm build passed and all 108 tests passed, including package install/activation coverage.

patch.value: Replaced obsolete SDK reader with listAllItemMetadata and verified the package against the pm-cli PR build with 108 passing tests.

author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary

patch.value: Fixed by migrating the extension runtime to listAllItemMetadata.
Published pm-changelog activates against the renamed pm-cli SDK and its commands remain available.
Source and dist artifacts use listAllItemMetadata; local pm-cli package activation and all 108 package tests pass.

message: Completed issue-resolution evidence after close validation warning.

author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary
message: Reopened for two-phase ecosystem release: published pm-cli lacks the new export, so a temporary import alias bridge is required for one pm-changelog release; final cleanup follows pm-cli merge/release.

value.author: codex-frontmatter-vocabulary
value.text: Bridge correction: the first transition release used a named legacy SDK import, which new pm-cli correctly rejects before aliasing. Replaced it with namespace feature detection that prefers listAllItemMetadata and only constructs the published fallback name at runtime; this supports both rollout sides without restoring an obsolete pm-cli export.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Continuation scope: Sentry PM-CLI-2B confirms the standalone generator can fail with spawnSync pm ENOENT when PATH lacks the pm shim. Extending this canonical SDK-reader lineage to use the installed @unbrained/pm-cli package executable contract while preserving explicit --pm-bin overrides.

value.note: Resolve installed pm-cli executable without PATH

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Evidence: default readPmItems now resolves @unbrained/pm-cli/package.json, validates its declared pm bin, and invokes the absolute CLI with process.execPath; explicit pmBin remains unchanged. Empty-PATH regression passed. npm run release:check passed: typecheck, 130 tests, zero production vulnerabilities, package dry-run, and current changelog.

patch.value: Resolved SDK/executable compatibility lineage: extension metadata uses the SDK and standalone reads no longer depend on a PATH shim; full release gate passed.

author: codex
message: Reopen for PR #76 review corrections: remove PATH entirely in regression coverage and harden cached installed pm-cli resolution errors.

value.author: codex
value.text: PR #76 review correction evidence: accepted Sourcery's absent-PATH regression and Gemini's contextual error handling suggestions. Default pm executable resolution is now cached, validates and reports the resolved manifest/bin payload, preserves the original cause, and the regression deletes PATH entirely without any. Hound ES6 import findings are false positives because this package is an ESM TypeScript project. npm run release:check passed typecheck, 130/130 tests, zero production vulnerabilities, package dry-run, and current changelog.

patch.value: Hardened and cached installed pm-cli resolution with contextual errors and a truly absent-PATH regression; 130 tests and all package release gates pass.
